plain_definition: Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission licenses different kinds of financial business under numbered categories called regulated activities -- for example, Type 1 covers dealing in securities, Type 4 covers advising on securities, Type 7 covers automated trading services, and Type 9 covers asset management. Hong Kong's new virtual asset licensing regimes largely reuse these same numbered categories and their existing requirements as a template.
formal_definition: A regulated activity listed in Schedule 5 of the Securities and Futures Ordinance (Cap. 571), each requiring its own SFC licence or registration.
